The published Weaviate/Cornell research paper is titled 'ACORN: Performant and Predicate-Agnostic Search over Vector Embeddings and Structured Data' — 'ACORN' is the algorithm's name, not an acronym for 'Adaptive Collaborative Retrieval Over Neighbor-links'. Candidates who recite this expansion in an interview and are challenged will have no source to cite.
issues.fix: ACORN (from the paper 'Performant and Predicate-Agnostic Search over Vector Embeddings and Structured Data')

vectordb.note: 94 cards audited (including all main q/a bodies, fj/fs pairs, and 219 followups). The file is generally high-quality — the core conceptual content on HNSW, IVF, ANN recall mechanics, metadata filtering strategies, and system comparisons is accurate and internally consistent. Four substantive issues found: one wrong data type in a memory formula (card 6 followup), one materially wrong MTEB benchmark score pair (card 66), one code-comment arithmetic error (card 90), and one likely-fabricated acronym expansion (card 44). No polarity issues detected — fj/fs pairs are correctly oriented throughout.

issues.claim: Genesys AI (formerly Predictive Engagement, now including Genesys Dialog Engine Bot Flows and integration with external LLMs)
issues.problem: Genesys Predictive Engagement is a separate Genesys product (a proactive digital-engagement tool that identifies high-value web visitors and triggers real-time outreach). It was not renamed or consolidated into 'Genesys AI'. Saying Genesys AI is 'formerly Predictive Engagement' conflates two distinct product lines and will confuse anyone who encounters Predictive Engagement as a live SKU in Genesys Cloud.
issues.fix: Genesys AI (the umbrella brand for Genesys Cloud AI capabilities, including Dialog Engine Bot Flows and integration with external LLMs)

issues.claim: ASR output is passed through a PII detector (AWS Comprehend Medical / Presidio) before entering the LLM context. Account numbers, SSNs, CCNs are replaced with tokens like [ACCOUNT_NUMBER]
issues.problem: AWS Comprehend Medical is purpose-built for extracting medical and clinical entities (diagnoses, medications, procedures, PHI in healthcare text). It does not detect general PII such as SSNs, credit card numbers, or account numbers. General PII detection in AWS uses the standard AWS Comprehend DetectPiiEntities API. Recommending Comprehend Medical for a generic contact-center PII masking pipeline is incorrect and would leave SSNs and payment data undetected in non-healthcare transcripts.
issues.fix: ASR output is passed through a PII detector (AWS Comprehend DetectPiiEntities / Presidio) before entering the LLM context. Account numbers, SSNs, CCNs are replaced with tokens like [ACCOUNT_NUMBER]

issues.claim: Voice cloning tools (ElevenLabs, VALL-E 2) can replicate a target voice from short samples publicly available on social media.
issues.problem: VALL-E 2 (Microsoft Research, June 2024) is an academic research paper and model that was never released as a publicly accessible voice-cloning service or attack tool. Listing it alongside ElevenLabs as a practical threat vector available to attackers is misleading. Real-world attacker tools include ElevenLabs, Resemble.ai, and open-source models such as OpenVoice or RVC, not VALL-E 2, which requires internal Microsoft access.
issues.fix: Voice cloning tools (ElevenLabs, Resemble.ai, and open-source models such as OpenVoice) can replicate a target voice from short samples publicly available on social media.

issues.claim: Genesys's native Agent Assist uses Google CCAI Insights
issues.problem: As of 2024-2025 Genesys has transitioned its native Agent Assist to Genesys Agent Copilot (powered by Genesys AI Experience), not Google CCAI Insights. The Google CCAI-based Genesys integration was part of an older partnership that Genesys has moved away from in favor of its own AI stack. Presenting this as the current production architecture is outdated.

issues.claim:
llm = anthropic.Anthropic()
        async with llm.messages.stream(model='claude-sonnet-4-5', messages=messages, max_tokens=512) as stream:
issues.problem: anthropic.Anthropic() is the synchronous client. Its messages.stream() returns a synchronous context manager; using it with 'async with' will raise a TypeError at runtime. An async function that awaits on the stream requires the async client: anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(). This is a breaking API error — the code will not run.
issues.fix:
llm = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()
        async with llm.messages.stream(model='claude-sonnet-4-5', messages=messages, max_tokens=512) as stream:

issues.claim: async for transcript in dg.listen.asynclive(audio_stream, LiveOptions(model='nova-2')):
issues.problem: In Deepgram Python SDK v3, dg.listen.asynclive is a class accessor, not a callable that accepts (audio_stream, LiveOptions) and returns an async iterator. The correct v3 pattern is event-callback-based: you obtain a connection via dg.listen.asynclive.v('1'), register handlers with connection.on(LiveTranscriptionEvents.Transcript, handler), then call await connection.start(options) and send audio chunks via await connection.send(). Iterating over dg.listen.asynclive(audio_stream, ...) will throw AttributeError.

voice-ai.note: 52 top-level cards + 34 followup cards = 86 total reviewed. Two issues are in the same code block of card 39 (voice loop prototype): wrong Anthropic client class and wrong Deepgram SDK v3 streaming pattern — both are runtime-breaking. The AWS Comprehend Medical mis-recommendation (card 19) is the most operationally dangerous factual error: a team following it would deploy PII masking that silently skips SSNs and payment card numbers in non-healthcare call transcripts. The Genesys 'formerly Predictive Engagement' branding error (card 2) and the VALL-E 2 attacker-tool claim (card 11 followup) are misleading but not catastrophically wrong. The Genesys Agent Assist / Google CCAI claim (card 30) is flagged as outdated with medium confidence — the partnership shift is directionally clear but exact current state should be confirmed against Genesys product docs before fixing.
